channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Avalanche buries 3 skiers near Anchorage, Alaska officials say
item.description: GIRDWOOD, Alaska (AP) — Three skiers who had flown by helicopter to a remote mountain range were swept away by an avalanche and likely killed under the weight of dozens of feet of snow, Alaska State Troopers said Wednesday. If the deaths are confirmed, it would be the deadliest U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump administration moves to drop Idaho emergency abortion case with national implications
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Wednesday moved to drop an Idaho emergency abortion case in one of its first moves on the issue since President Donald Trump began his second term.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Steve Carell surprises Southern California high school students with free prom tickets
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Actor Steve Carell is sending Southern California high school students affected by the devasting wildfires in Los Angeles to the prom for free.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration deletes list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 440 federal properties it had identified to potentially offload, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republicans hammer mayors of Boston, Chicago, Denver and New York over 'sanctuary city' policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican members of Congress hammered four Democratic mayors Wednesday about their so-called sanctuary city policies, accusing them of endangering Americans and threatening to prosecute local officials.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Scientists raise concerns as the US stops sharing air quality data from embassies worldwide
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The U.S. government will stop sharing air quality data gathered from its embassies and consulates, worrying local scientists and experts who say the effort was vital to monitor global air quality and improve public health.
item.dc:creator: TAIWO ADEBAYO and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: As many top Democrats stay silent on Cuomo mayoral run, an accuser feels betrayed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lindsey Boylan helped drive Andrew Cuomo from office in 2021 when the former aide came forward as the first woman to accuse the governor of sexual harassment.
item.dc:creator: BY JAKE OFFENHARTZ and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The controversy behind California's swastika-shaped building complex
item.description: The Navy even went public on its website with a statement regarding the buildings, saying it understands their configuration is a "sensitive topic.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court allows removal of watchdog agency head as legal battle rages over Trump firing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An appeals court in Washington on Wednesday removed the head of a federal watchdog agency in the latest twist in a legal fight over Republican President Donald Trump's authority to fire the special counsel. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Vance suggests other industries won't get tariff carve-outs
item.description: Vice President Vance on Wednesday said that industries other than U.S. automakers have asked for exemptions on tariffs, but suggested that the Trump administration would not extend further carve outs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump grants one-month exemption for US automakers from new tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is granting a one-month exemption on his stiff new tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada for U.S. automakers, as worries persist that the newly launched trade war could crush domestic manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Three Michigan brothers missing since 2010 are declared dead by a judge at mom's request
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — A judge declared three Michigan brothers dead Wednesday, more than 14 years after they disappeared at Thanksgiving in a small-town tragedy that remains unsolved despite an explicit belief by investigators that their father is responsible.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The US is killing someone by firing squad for the 1st time in 15 years. Here's a look at the history
item.description: It was a punishment for mutiny in colonial times, a way to discourage desertion during the Civil War and a dose of frontier justice in the Old West. In modern times, some consider it a more humane alternative to lethal injection. The firing squad has a long and thorny history in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mysterious affliction causing endangered Florida fish to spin and die is back
item.description: Following a roughly nine-month lull, there have been 22 reports of the odd spinning behavior involving sawfish in waters around the Florida Keys since December.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michigan brothers missing since 2010 declared dead at mom's request
item.description: A judge declared three Michigan brothers dead Wednesday, more than 14 years after they disappeared at Thanksgiving in a small-town tragedy that remains unsolved.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boy reportedly kidnapped from Atlanta 7 years ago found in Colorado; 2 arrested
item.description: A 14-year-old boy who went missing seven years ago has been located, some 1,400 miles from where he was allegedly abducted.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, WGN 9

item.title: Watch: Fishermen rush to save 900-pound dolphin that landed on their boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 900 pounds appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump pleads with people of Greenland to join the US
item.description: (The Hill) - President Trump on Tuesday said he’d welcome the people of Greenland to join the U.S. as he stressed that “I think we’re going to get” the self-governed Arctic territory within Denmark. "I also have a message tonight for the incredible people of Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: US paused Ukraine intelligence support: Trump officials
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe and national security adviser Michael Waltz both said the U.S. had paused intelligence sharing in addition to military aid.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WGN 9

item.title: Powerful US storms kill 2 and bring threats from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms killed two people in Mississippi, tore the roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in a small town in Oklahoma and threatened more communities across the nation Tuesday with wide-ranging weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lutnick: Trump may announce tariff compromise with Canada, Mexico on Wednesday
item.description: Trump implementing 25 percent tariffs triggered retaliatory measures from both nations, with major corporations also warning that it could lead to potential price increases for consumers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Jimmy John's is giving out free sandwiches. Here's how to redeem the limited-time deal
item.description: Sandwich chain Jimmy John’s is giving away $1 million worth of toasted subs on Tuesday to celebrate a new product launch.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Idaho murders case: Bryan Kohberger judge ends 'pervasive' secret filings restricting what public can see
item.description: Judge overseeing Bryan Kohberger's Idaho student murders case says practice of filing many documents under seal violates the First Amendment and will end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Royal Caribbean cruise passenger goes overboard on ‘80s-themed voyage, family says
item.description: A woman is presumed dead after going overboard on a Royal Caribbean ship during the first day of the '80s-themed cruise near the first port in the Bahamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Columbia professor slams university leadership as anti-Israel agitators wreak havoc at Barnard
item.description: Columbia associate professor Shai Davidai criticizes leadership at Columbia and Barnard as anti-Israel agitators once again wreak havoc on campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Red Robin considering closing dozens of 'underperforming' restaurants
item.description: Red Robin, which has locations in all but five states, says it's reviewing dozens of "underperforming" stores for potential closure.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans target 4 'sanctuary' cities as Trump pushes mass deportations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans in Congress are taking aim at four cities — often called “sanctuary cities” — over their policies limiting cooperation with immigration enforcement with a hearing this week that comes as President Donald Trump presses ahead with his campaign of mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pope Francis no longer requires mechanical ventilation after respiratory crisis
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis had stabilized enough Tuesday after two respiratory crises to be taken off noninvasive mechanical ventilation and receive just high flows of supplemental oxygen, as he continued his more than two-week long fight against double pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US tariffs on Canada, Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico went into effect Tuesday, putting global markets on edge and setting up costly retaliations by the United States’ North American allies.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, PAUL WISEMAN and ROB GILLIES Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump pauses aid to Ukraine amid clash with Zelensky
item.description: President Trump has ordered a pause on U.S. aid going to Ukraine in its war against Russia, a consequential move as he clashes with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky about the path to ending the conflict. "The President has been clear that he is focused on peace.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Trump: 25 percent tariffs on Canada and Mexico will go into effect Tuesday
item.description: President Trump said Monday that the 25 percent tariffs on Canada and Mexico will officially go into effect Tuesday, announcing there is no room for dealmaking to delay them again. “The tariffs, they're all set.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two new episodes of acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Threatening letter sent to northern Colorado family over MAGA banner
item.description: A Severance family says they recently received a threatening letter in the mail, in response to a "Make America Great Again" banner posted in their front yard.
item.dc:creator: Greg Nieto, ABC 4
